title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Uterine Fibroids
intro: Herbal teas for Uterine fibroids are a natural remedy that involves consuming tea made from various herbs to alleviate the symptoms of uterine fibroids. These teas work by reducing inflammation, promoting menstrual regulation, and improving circulation to the uterus, thus helping to shrink the fibroids. Some examples of herbal teas used to treat uterine fibroids include chamomile tea, which helps to reduce pain and stress; raspberry leaf tea, which regulates menstrual cycles and eases cramps; dandelion root tea, which stimulates liver function and reduces bloating; peppermint tea, which soothes digestive issues and reduces inflammation; and red clover tea, which helps to balance estrogen levels and shrink fibroids. Additionally, other herbal teas such as licorice root, ginger, and turmeric are also used to treat uterine fibroids due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the licorice root, have been traditionally used to treat uterine fibroids due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-proliferative properties. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, including glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and saponins, have been found to inhibit the growth of fibroid cells and reduce inflammation in the uterus. By regulating the balance of hormones and reducing oxidative stress, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas may help alleviate the symptoms of uterine fibroids, such as heavy bleeding and pelvic pain. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for uterine fibroids treatment include reduced risk of surgical interventions, improved quality of life, and natural hormonal regulation.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Place the dried root in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dried root.
Let the tea steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, for 3-6 months for relief from uterine fibroids symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat uterine fibroids, but it may cause side effects such as increased blood pressure, potassium loss, and water retention due to its high glycyrrhizin content. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas safely, it is recommended to drink them in moderation and limit the consumption to 1-2 cups per day, as excessive use can exacerbate the side effects and lead to complications such as heart problems and insomnia.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional for a personalized treatment plan.
Drink 2-3 cups of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea per day for 2-3 months.
Start with 1 tablespoon of dried root per cup of boiling water, then adjust as needed.
Take 2-3 times a day, preferably after meals, with a glass of water.
Monitor your symptoms and adjust your dosage under healthcare professional guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Vitex agnus-castus teas have been used to treat uterine fibroids due to their antiproliferative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to inhibit the growth of fibroid cells and reduce inflammation in the uterus. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which work together to regulate hormonal balance and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids. By stimulating the production of dopamine and reducing prolactin levels, Vitex agnus-castus teas can help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms such as heavy bleeding and pelvic pain. The benefits of using this herbal preparation to treat uterine fibroids include reduced symptoms, improved hormonal balance, and a lower risk of complications associated with surgery or other treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Vitex agnus-castus leaves and flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the leaves and flowers and place them in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Add 1 cup of boiling water over the Vitex agnus-castus leaves and flowers.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally on an empty stomach, for maximum benefit.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of the American Pharmaceutical Association (Washington, D.C. : 1996)", Vitex agnus-castus teas may be considered a potential treatment alternative for patients with uterine fibroids due to its traditional use in treating conditions such as premenstrual syndrome (PMS), although further investigation is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for this indication.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vitex agnus-castus teas can cause dizziness, headaches, and nausea in some individuals when used to treat uterine fibroids, as well as interact with other medications and worsen underlying conditions such as high blood pressure. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose, monitor blood pressure regularly, and avoid using Vitex agnus-castus teas during pregnancy or if breastfeeding, as well as while taking medications that affect blood clotting.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Vitex agnus-castus tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons if symptoms persist after 2 weeks.
Continue using tea for 3-6 months to see noticeable improvements.
Consult a healthcare professional for guidance and monitoring.

preparations.herb_desc: Angelica sinensis teas, also known as dong quai, have been traditionally used to treat uterine fibroids due to its properties as a uterine tonic and its ability to regulate menstrual cycles. The bioactive constituents of Angelica sinensis, including ferulic acid, ferulic esters, and coumarins, help to inhibit the growth of fibroid cells and reduce inflammation. Additionally, the herbal preparation's anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate symptoms associated with fibroids, such as heavy menstrual bleeding and pelvic pain. The benefits of using Angelica sinensis teas to treat uterine fibroids include reduced fibroid size, improved menstrual regularity, and a decrease in symptoms associated with the condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 10 grams of dried Angelica sinensis root. You can buy it from a health food store or online.
Heat 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot.
Add 2 teaspoons of the dried Angelica sinensis root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Angelica sinensis teas can be used to treat uterine fibroids, but it may cause side effects such as increased menstrual bleeding, dizziness, and nausea in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using these teas, as they may interact with blood-thinning medications and exacerbate conditions such as high blood pressure, and pregnant women should avoid consuming them altogether.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Angelica sinensis tea daily for 3-6 months.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of Angelica sinensis powder per cup of boiling water.
Use fresh Angelica sinensis herbs for better results and flavor.
Drink tea 3 times a week for maintenance and prevention.
Monitor fibroid size and symptoms, adjust dosage if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat uterine fibroids due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the size and alleviate the symptoms of fibroids. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, which help to inhibit the growth of fibroid cells and promote hormonal balance. By reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow to the uterus, Ginkgo biloba teas can help to shrink fibroids and alleviate symptoms such as heavy menstrual bleeding and pelvic pressure. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas may also help to prevent the recurrence of fibroids and promote overall uterine health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Ginkgo biloba leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Combine the Ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the liquid, if desired, to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for uterine fibroid relief, ideally 30 minutes before meals.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can help alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids, but potential side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to the herbal compounds interacting with medications or exacerbating pre-existing conditions. Precautions when using Ginkgo biloba teas include avoiding them during pregnancy or breastfeeding, as well as limiting consumption to recommended amounts to minimize the risk of interactions with other substances.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day as needed.
Use 1-2 teaspoons dried Ginkgo biloba leaves per 1 cup boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes and then strain the tea.
Consume tea for 3-6 months to potentially reduce fibroid size.
Consult a healthcare professional for dosage and frequency adjustments.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as nettle root tea, have been traditionally used to treat uterine fibroids due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids, such as heavy bleeding and pelvic pain. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica tea, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans, are believed to help treat uterine fibroids by inhibiting the growth of fibroid cells and promoting hormone balance. The benefits of Urtica dioica tea in treating uterine fibroids include reduced symptoms, improved menstrual regularity, and enhanced overall reproductive health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves and flowers, or 2 teaspoons of dried leaves and flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a saucepan or kettle until it reaches a boil.
Add the gathered Urtica dioica to the hot water. If using fresh, add 1 cup. If using dried, add 2 teaspoons.
Reduce heat to low and let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the mixture and drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate uterine fibroid symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be used to help alleviate the symptoms of uterine fibroids, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions due to the plant's high histamine content. Precautions should be taken when using Urtica dioica teas, such as starting with small doses, avoiding it if you have sensitive skin or allergies, and being cautious if you have any underlying stomach or digestive issues.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional before using Urtica dioica tea for uterine fibroids.
Drink 1 cup of Urtica dioica tea 3 times a day for 6-8 weeks.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Urtica dioica leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink as a tea.
Monitor your condition and adjust dosage or discontinue use if symptoms worsen.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas have been traditionally used to treat uterine fibroids due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-fibrotic properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, such as flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, help to inhibit the growth of fibroid cells and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms associated with uterine fibroids. The anti-fibrotic properties of Silybum marianum teas are particularly beneficial in treating uterine fibroids, as they help to prevent the formation of scar tissue and promote the degradation of existing fibroids. Regular consumption of Silybum marianum teas has been reported to provide relief from symptoms such as heavy menstrual bleeding, pelvic pressure, and abdominal pain, making it a promising natural remedy for uterine fibroids.

Measure 1 teaspoon of the dried flowers and leaves into a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the herbs and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally between meals.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to potentially treat uterine fibroids, but its use may lead to side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Silybum marianum teas to treat uterine fibroids, it is recommended to start with a low dose, monitor liver function, and avoid taking it during pregnancy or breastfeeding, as well as with other medications that may interact with its compounds.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Silybum marianum tea for uterine fibroids.
Drink 1 cup of Silybum marianum tea 3 times a day for 3-6 months.
Start with a low dosage of 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.

Monitor your condition and adjust the dosage or consult your doctor if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Trifolium pratense teas, also known as red clover teas, have been traditionally used to help alleviate the symptoms of uterine fibroids due to their anti-inflammatory and antifibrotic properties. These teas contain isoflavones, which help to regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce the growth of uterine fibroids by inhibiting the proliferation of fibroblasts. The bioactive constituents of red clover teas, including coumarins and flavonoids, have been shown to have estrogen-like effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids, such as heavy menstrual bleeding and pelvic pain. Regular consumption of Trifolium pratense teas may help to reduce the size of uterine fibroids and alleviate symptoms, providing relief to women suffering from this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Trifolium pratense flowers (red clover).
Combine the dried flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Add honey to taste, if desired. Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 3 times a day.
Store any leftover tea in the fridge for up to 3 days.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Trifolium pratense teas can be used to treat uterine fibroids, but its consumption may lead to side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach cramps due to its potent saponin and isoflavone content. To use Trifolium pratense teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses, monitor blood pressure and hormonal levels, and avoid consuming it during pregnancy or breastfeeding to minimize potential complications.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Trifolium pratense teas for uterine fibroids
Drink 1 cup of Trifolium pratense tea 3 times a day for 6 weeks
Maintain a consistent dosage of 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup
Drink Trifolium pratense tea with meals to aid digestion and absorption
Monitor your condition and adjust dosage or consult a doctor after 6 weeks

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat uterine fibroids due to their anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, Lavandula angustifolia teas can help to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with uterine fibroids, thereby improving the overall quality of life for those affected. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat uterine fibroids include reduced symptoms, improved menstrual cycle regulation, and a decrease in the risk of complications associated with this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers. You can buy them online or in health stores.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the dried flowers and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop. Let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Pour the boiled water over the dried flowers in the tea infuser or cup. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the flowers. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat uterine fibroids, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and headaches due to the essential oils present in the tea, which can cause uterine contractions and interact with certain medications. Precautions should be taken to avoid consuming Lavandula angustifolia teas in large quantities or for extended periods, as this may lead to an imbalance of hormones and worsen the condition, and also be aware of potential allergic reactions to the essential oils.

preparations.herb_desc: Paeonia lactiflora teas have been traditionally used to treat uterine fibroids due to their anti-inflammatory and anti-estrogenic properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing the size and number of fibroids, alleviating symptoms such as heavy menstrual bleeding and pelvic pain. The bioactive constituents of Paeonia lactiflora teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the growth of fibroid cells and regulating hormonal imbalances. Regular consumption of Paeonia lactiflora teas may also help to reduce the risk of fibroid recurrence and improve overall uterine health, making it a beneficial natural remedy for managing uterine fibroids.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Paeonia lactiflora root, 2 cups of water, and a tea infuser.
Place the dried Paeonia lactiflora root in the tea infuser.
Heat the water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Pour the boiling water over the Paeonia lactiflora root in the tea infuser and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1-2 cups per day, ideally before meals.
preparations.preparation_study: According to The American journal of Chinese medicine, Paeonia lactiflora teas for uterine fibroids may be beneficial, as research suggests that Paeonia lactiflora, a component of the traditional Chinese medicine Tokishakuyakusan (TJ-23), may help stimulate the secretion of progesterone, which is involved in the regulation of the corpus luteum and may have a positive effect on uterine fibroids.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Paeonia lactiflora teas can be used to treat uterine fibroids, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and digestive issues due to the saponins and isoflavones present in the tea. preparations.herb_desc: Crataegus monogyna teas have been traditionally used to treat uterine fibroids due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce the size of fibroids, alleviate symptoms such as heavy menstrual bleeding and pelvic pain, and improve overall health. The bioactive constituents of Crataegus monogyna teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Regular consumption of Crataegus monogyna teas may also help to reduce the risk of fibroid recurrence and alleviate symptoms associated with menopause, making it a beneficial herbal preparation for women suffering from uterine fibroids.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Crataegus monogyna leaves. If fresh, use 2 cups of leaves and dry them in a low oven (150°F) for 2 hours.
Combine the dried leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth. Discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and mix well. Some people find the taste bitter, so the honey can help.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, 2-3 times a day. You can store the tea in the fridge for up to 3 days.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Crataegus monogyna teas can potentially cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions when used to treat uterine fibroids, particularly if consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include monitoring blood pressure and uterine contractions, as well as avoiding consumption during pregnancy or breastfeeding due to potential effects on the fetus or baby.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas have been traditionally used to treat uterine fibroids due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and estrogen-modulating properties. These teas help to alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the uterus, thereby shrinking the fibroids and improving overall uterine health. The bioactive constituents of Avena sativa, including avenanthramides and avenacosides, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the growth of fibroid cells and promoting apoptosis (cell death). Regular consumption of Avena sativa teas has been found to provide relief from symptoms such as heavy menstrual bleeding, pelvic pain, and anemia associated with uterine fibroids.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Avena sativa root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea strainer.
Add the Avena sativa root to the tea strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the Avena sativa root in the strainer.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then carefully pour it into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally 30 minutes before meals, for 2-3 months.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be used to treat uterine fibroids, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Avena sativa teas, including avoiding prolonged use, as it may lead to dependence, and being cautious with its interaction with other medications, especially blood thinners and sedatives.

By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Ulmus rubra teas can help to alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids, including heavy menstrual bleeding and pelvic pain. The benefits of using Ulmus rubra teas to treat uterine fibroids include reduced symptoms, improved quality of life, and a natural alternative to conventional treatments.

The bioactive constituents of Cassia auriculata, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, which help to shrink fibroid tumors and improve overall uterine health. Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help with uterine fibroids. Consult a doctor before using it as a treatment.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytotherapy research : PTR, Cassia auriculata teas are not recommended for uterine fibroids or any other conditions during pregnancy due to potential abortifacient and/or teratogenic activity.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cassia auriculata teas can be used as a potential treatment for uterine fibroids, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal disturbances, nausea, and vomiting due to its harsh effects on the digestive system. When using Cassia auriculata teas to treat uterine fibroids, it is recommended to be cautious, as excessive consumption may lead to liver damage, allergic reactions, or interactions with other medications, and it's best to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat uterine fibroids due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids, such as pain and heavy bleeding. By modulating the prostaglandin levels in the body, ginger tea helps to reduce the size and growth of uterine fibroids, thereby providing relief from the discomfort and distress caused by this condition. The benefits of using ginger tea to treat uterine fibroids include reduced pain, improved menstrual cycle regulation, and a lower risk of complications associated with the condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh ginger root, 2 cups of water, and a strainer.
Peel the ginger root and slice it into thin pieces.
Boil the 2 cups of water in a pot and add the sliced ginger root.
Reduce the heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1/2 cup of the ginger tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate uterine fibroid symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the given study, there is no direct information on the effectiveness of Zingiber officinale teas for uterine fibroids, but it does mention Zingiber officinale (Ginger) as one of the 571 ethnic medicines commonly used for women's health issues.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used as a natural remedy to alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its high ginger content. To avoid potential complications, it is recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase the amount, as well as to consume the tea in moderation, especially during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and to be aware of any allergic reactions or interactions with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas, a traditional herbal preparation, have been studied for their potential to treat uterine fibroids due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents present in Curcuma longa teas, such as curcumin and demethoxycurcumin, help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to the growth and development of uterine fibroids. This herbal preparation may help to alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids, including heavy menstrual bleeding and pelvic pain, by modulating the immune system and preventing the proliferation of fibroid cells. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat uterine fibroids include reduced risk of complications, improved quality of life, and potential avoidance of surgical interventions.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Place the Curcuma longa root in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Curcuma longa root.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of the Curcuma longa tea per day, ideally 3 times a day, for up to 3 months.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytotherapy research : PTR, Curcuma longa teas for uterine fibroids have no specific effects mentioned in the provided study, as it only mentions the high percentage of clinical research on Curcuma longa and its antioxidant properties, but does not specify its use for uterine fibroids.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be beneficial in treating uterine fibroids due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach ulcers, as well as an increased risk of bleeding. To minimize these side effects, it is essential to consume Curcuma longa teas in moderation, avoid excessive consumption, and consider the timing of consumption in relation to meals and other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Cynara scolymus teas, also known as artichoke teas, have been traditionally used to treat uterine fibroids due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These herbal preparations help to treat uterine fibroids by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to the growth and development of fibroids. The bioactive constituents of Cynara scolymus teas, including cynarin and silymarin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fibroid cells and promote apoptosis, or cell death. Regular consumption of Cynara scolymus teas has been reported to reduce the size and number of uterine fibroids, providing relief from symptoms such as heavy bleeding and pelvic pain.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of fresh or dried Cynara scolymus leaves. Fresh leaves can be found in some health food stores or grown at home.
Wash the leaves with water to remove any dirt or debris. Pat them dry with a paper towel.
Put 2 tablespoons of the leaves in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. If using a cup, you can tie the leaves with a string.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the leaves. You can add honey or lemon to taste. Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cynara scolymus teas can be used as a potential herbal remedy to treat uterine fibroids, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use this herbal preparation safely, it is essential to monitor your body's response and discontinue use if you experience severe or persistent side effects, and also be cautious when taking it during pregnancy or breastfeeding.
